source.title: Unauthorized Release of Synthetic Based Mud - Shell Perdido Spar

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Alaminos Canyon Block 857

event.incident_type: Unauthorized discharge of drilling fluid to sea
actions_taken: Well was shut-in and circulated out through gas buster
Observer posted to watch for sheens for 30 minutes
Knife valve and butterfly valve removed and inspected
event.summary: On September 4, 2023, approximately 24 barrels of Synthetic Based Mud (SBM) were discharged into the Gulf of Mexico from the H&P 205 drilling rig on Shell's Perdido Spar. The crew was circulating a clean out run and had transferred 117 bbls of SBM to processing pits to remove entrained gas. Seven minutes after the transfer pump was turned off, the degasser tank volume began decreasing. Investigation revealed that both the knife valve on the degasser tank discharge line and the butterfly valve (master dump valve) had leaked due to obstructions - cuttings in the knife valve and a piece of rubber in the butterfly valve - preventing proper sealing. The crew had visually verified valve positions per procedure but did not functionally test the valves prior to transfer. The valves had not been operated since April 2023.
recommendations: Crews will fill processing pits with sea water to check for any leaks through containment valves prior to future operations
key_phrases: no skillet on processing pit master dump valve

hazards.name: Synthetic Based Mud under pressure in processing pits

consequences.name: Environmental pollution - discharge to Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Knife valve on degasser tank discharge line
controls.barrier_role: Containment valve to prevent discharge from degasser tank
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier to prevent fluid release

supporting_text: The knife valve located on the discharge line of the degasser tank...was found to have been obstructed by cuttings keeping it from seating and sealing properly
The knife valve is also located overhead at a height of about 12 feet, the blade position may have been difficult to verify given the height

controls.name: Butterfly valve (master dump valve for processing pits)
controls.barrier_role: Master isolation valve to prevent discharge from processing tanks
controls.lod_basis: Secondary physical barrier providing redundant containment

supporting_text: The butterfly valve used as a master dump valve for the discharge lines connected to the processing tanks were found to have leaked
The butterfly valve was observed to have a piece of rubber of an unknown origin preventing full closure
The butterfly valve was previously locked in the closed position after the processing tanks were cleaned out

controls.name: Visual valve alignment check by two people
controls.barrier_role: Verification of valve position prior to fluid transfer
controls.lod_basis: Procedural check to ensure proper valve configuration before operations

supporting_text: The H&P Fluid Transfer Procedure states, 'prior to transferring fluids, the valve configuration is to be visually checked by 2 people.'
The H&P Pit-Hand, and the H&P Assistant Driller (AD) both walked down the line up and checked the valve alignments
Human Performance Error. Complacency, H&P crew only used visual indicators to verify the valves were closed

controls.name: Functional testing of valves prior to fluid transfer
controls.barrier_role: Verification of valve operability and sealing capability
controls.lod_basis: Procedural verification to ensure valves can perform their containment function

supporting_text: Both valves had not been functioned and operated immediately prior to the fluid transfer
The last time the valves were functioned was April 2023
Equipment Failure, inadequate equipment testing and inspection. Lessee failed to operate the degasser tank outlet and the master dump valves thus verifing their ability to seal

controls.name: Pit level monitoring and volume indicators
controls.barrier_role: Detection of abnormal fluid loss from tanks
controls.lod_basis: Monitoring system to detect loss of containment

supporting_text: Approximately 7 minutes after the transfer pump was turned off, the H&P Driller noticed the volume indicator for the degasser tank was decreasing
The driller monitored the pit level sensor for a minute to see if the volume was just leveling out, but when it kept dropping, he contacted the H&P Rig Manager

controls.name: Skillet on master dump valve
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent unintentional discharge
controls.lod_basis: Secondary physical containment to catch leaks from master dump valve

supporting_text: A skillet is installed on the active pit master dump valve to prevent unintentional discharge but, there was no skillet on the processing pit master dump valve (butterfly valve)

Management of Change Procedures. The active pits master dump valve has a skillet installed to prevent unintentional discharge. The processing and reserve do not have a skillet installed at the master dump valve. This would have prevented the unintentional release.

controls.name: Lock-out on butterfly valve
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent unauthorized valve operation
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to ensure only authorized personnel can operate critical valves

supporting_text: The butterfly valve was previously locked in the closed position after the processing tanks were cleaned out
The Tool-pusher and the Rig Manager are the only personnel with access to the key and authority to remove the lock
The H&P Pit-hand and H&P AD also verified the butterfly valve was closed and in the locked position

controls.name: Observer posted to watch for sheens
controls.barrier_role: Detection and monitoring of environmental impact
controls.lod_basis: Post-release monitoring to assess environmental consequences

supporting_text: Once the H&P drilling crew identified that the fluid was released into the Gulf of Mexico, the H&P Rig Manager posted an observer to watch for any sheens caused by the SBM discharge
The observer watched for 30 minutes and did not identify any sheens resulting from the discharge

controls.name: Processing pit covers
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent debris entry into processing pits
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier to prevent contamination of fluid system

supporting_text: Processing Pits are of the open type and the processing pit covers have gaps that may allow debris to enter the system

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
